steps.title: Use two points from the graph
steps.content: The graphed line clearly passes through $(-2,-3)$ and $(0,9)$. The slope is $$\frac{9-(-3)}{0-(-2)}=\frac{12}{2}=6,$$ so the line has equation $y=6x+9$.

steps.title: Build the equation
steps.content: Rewrite the equation so it matches the answer choices. From $y=6x+9$, multiply by 2 to make the constant 18: $$2y=12x+18$$ Move the $12x$ term to the left: $$-12x+2y=18$$ This has the form $-12x+Ry=18$ with $R=2$.

steps.title: Match the positive constant
steps.content: Since $R=2$ is positive, $-12x+Ry=18$ is allowed. That is the matching answer, so D is correct.
